name: Expectancy & Asymmetry
description: How Matrix evaluates every setup — maximize long-term expectancy, not highest probability.
experimentHypothesis: Emphasizing maximum achievable asymmetry (vs waiting for stronger confirmation) improves long-term capital growth across a large statistical sample.
principles: Maximize long-term expectancy — not highest probability
Evaluate thesis quality AND opportunity quality — neither alone is sufficient
Confirmation has a cost — stronger thesis can mean weaker opportunity
Maximum achievable R — not maximum theoretical R
Losses are normal — average winners must outweigh average losers
One trade never validates or invalidates the Playbook

methodology.philosophy: Matrix does not seek the highest probability trade. Matrix seeks the highest long-term expectancy. The objective is to maximize capital growth through asymmetric opportunities while keeping losses predefined and repeatable. Every trade is one observation within a large statistical sample. No individual trade validates or invalidates the Playbook.
methodology.corePrinciple: Expected return is determined by both probability of success and reward relative to risk. Increasing probability is not always beneficial if it significantly reduces available Reward:Risk. Matrix evaluates whether the thesis is improving AND whether the remaining opportunity is still economically attractive.
methodology.asymmetryPrinciple: The preferred trade is not necessarily the safest trade. It is the one offering the greatest achievable asymmetry while maintaining predefined risk, structural validity, and realistic execution. Maximum theoretical R is not the objective — maximum achievable R is.
methodology.confirmationCost: Confirmation is not free. Waiting may increase entry price, reduce remaining upside, widen effective risk, and lower Reward:Risk. A stronger thesis can coexist with a weaker investment opportunity. The Scouting Desk must evaluate both Thesis Quality and Opportunity Quality.
methodology.opportunityPreservation: When price reaches a predefined strategic area, delaying execution should be justified by a measurable improvement in expectancy. If additional confirmation is expected to reduce Reward:Risk below Playbook standards, the opportunity may be deteriorating despite increasing confidence.
methodology.statisticalFramework: Matrix assumes losses are normal. The objective is not avoiding losses — it is ensuring average winners significantly outweigh average losers. A strategy with larger average Reward:Risk can remain profitable despite a modest win rate. Long-term consistency takes precedence over individual outcomes.
methodology.continuousLearning: Every completed trade contributes information. Winning trades validate execution. Losing trades provide statistical evidence. Neither should independently modify the Playbook. Playbook evolution requires repeated evidence across multiple qualified trades.

decisionPhilosophy: GO / WAIT / NO — maximize long-term expectancy rather than maximize certainty.
checklist: Thesis quality scored independently from opportunity quality
Current R:R meets minimum (Stock File or Playbook)
Estimated R:R after confirmation still acceptable if waiting
Predefined risk and structural stop — no manufactured R:R
Delay justified by measurable expectancy gain, not comfort
Outcome recorded as one statistical observation

name: Asymmetric Support Entry
description: Tactical application of Expectancy & Asymmetry at structural support zones.
principles: Apply expectancy-asymmetry framework at support zones
Location evidence may justify probe before full confirmation
Single entry when no meaningful add levels exist
checklist: Stock File zone defined with minimum R:R
Price inside or near planned strategic zone
Setup stop defined — acceptable dollar loss if stopped
